$comment: Authoritative source of record for the metrics-CSV integrity gate (spec 250, obstacle #257). This file — NOT prose in wiki/metrics/COUNTING.md — is the single copy of the 8-column header contract and the canonical event_type registry. The reader-detects gate binds to the values here; COUNTING.md points at this file. Do not duplicate the header or the registry anywhere else: a second copy is itself a drift vector (spec 110 soundness invariant — a duplicate false-REDs on a genuinely new name and reads green on a typo).

anchors.$comment: Typed anchors at columns 1 / 3 / 7 (1-based, matching the header positions the spec cites). Any count-preserving shift that displaces a typed column disturbs at least one anchor, closing the width-preserving hole that width+registry alone would miss.

date.description: Calendar day (YYYY-MM-DD), optionally followed by an ISO-8601 T-time suffix. A format-tolerant superset, never a single-format pin: the live corpus is calendar-day, but a future ISO timestamp must not false-RED.

value.description: Integer or decimal, optional leading minus.

event_type.description: Must equal one of the canonical machine names in eventTypeRegistry. Doubles as the column-7 typed anchor.

unconstrained.$comment: Columns intentionally NOT shape-checked. Documenting the exclusions is part of the contract — a future maintainer must know these are deliberate, not oversights.

note.reason: Free text — the only field that may legitimately carry a delimiter. A shape check here would false-RED on legitimate content and collide with the RFC-4180 quoting rule below.

host_run.reason: Legitimately empty, '-', a numeric run id, or occasional free text (e.g. 'storyboard-review'). Its looseness is the vulnerability the shape leg works around, not a checkable invariant — so it carries no anchor and needs none.

validRow.$comment: A row is valid if and only if ALL THREE legs hold. The gate fails RED — naming the offending file and line — on any violated leg (fail on NOT-A OR NOT-B OR NOT-C). The semantics are recorded here so this file is the single source; the boolean logic lives in the gate, which binds to the fields above.
validRow.legA_width: The row parses to exactly header.length (8) RFC-4180 fields, AND the file's header row matches `header` in order (the header is checked once, against this committed constant — never row-1 self-consistency, which a uniformly-dropped column would pass).
validRow.legB_registry: Column 7 (event_type) is a member of eventTypeRegistry.
validRow.legC_shape: Column 1 (date) matches anchors.date.pattern, AND column 3 (value) matches anchors.value.pattern.

quoting.$comment: RFC-4180. The reader MUST parse with a genuine CSV reader, never a naive split on every comma.
quoting.rule: Quote any note (column 6) that contains a comma, a double-quote, or a newline; escape an embedded double-quote by doubling it. #257's comma-only stopgap misses embedded-quote and newline breakage — RFC-4180 covers all three.
quoting.whyRealParse: A naive `awk -F,` / split-on-comma detector fails in lockstep with the analyzer: it shatters a quoted note on its embedded commas and bleeds note fragments into the event_type column — exactly the corruption this gate exists to catch. Only a real RFC-4180 parse can validate these rows.

provenance.grounding: The header and registry were grounded by a genuine csv.reader sweep of all 14 wiki/metrics/*/2026.csv files on 2026-07-22: the 8-column header is byte-identical across every file, and these six event_type names are the complete observed machine set.
provenance.registrySource: The harness emits these names; no upstream enum exists to derive from, and .github/workflows/*.yml filenames are not a clean derivation source (kata-shift and agent-skill have no same-named workflow; agent-coaching.yml and kata-interview.yml have no same-named event_type). This registry is therefore hand-authored. The fail-closed gate is what FORCES a genuinely new upstream name into this list by deliberate PR — that is the anti-drift property, not a gap.
provenance.realParseIsLoadBearing: A deliberately naive `awk -F,` sweep on 2026-07-22 reproduced the #257 corruption directly: quoted-note fragments such as ' awaiting human approval...' bled into the event_type column. This is the live proof that the real-parse invariant is load-bearing, not defensive.
provenance.updateDiscipline: When the harness begins emitting a new event_type, add it here in a deliberate PR — never widen the gate merely to silence a RED. Re-sweep the corpus and bump `lastReviewed` on every change. Registry entries are kept alphabetically sorted; the set, not the order, is what the gate tests.
